description: A content writer/strategist accumulating editorial knowledge. Questions are deliberately HARD: paraphrased (low lexical overlap with the memory, defeats BM25), multi-hop (answer needs two chained memories), and distractor-heavy (several memories share vocabulary). Designed so recall@10 does not saturate.

memories.text: The house style forbids the Oxford comma in body copy but requires it in legal disclaimers.
Long-form pieces are capped at 2,400 words; anything longer is split into a series.
The newsletter goes out Tuesday at 9am; the blog publishes Thursday. Social teasers trail each by one day.
Decision: we dropped the third-person brand voice for a first-person founder voice, because readers engaged 40% more with the founder's personal essays.
Every claim with a statistic must link a primary source; secondary aggregators are not acceptable citations.
The SEO target is one pillar page per quarter, each supported by six cluster posts that internally link up to it.
Guest contributors keep copyright but grant a perpetual, non-exclusive license; the contract template lives in the legal folder.
Image alt text is mandatory and must describe function, not appearance, for screen-reader users.
Headlines are A/B tested in the newsletter subject line before being reused on the blog.
We sunset evergreen posts after 18 months unless traffic stays above 500 visits/month, in which case we refresh rather than retire them.

questions.question: When may I use a serial comma?
questions.answer: Only in legal disclaimers — house style forbids the Oxford comma in body copy but requires it in disclaimers.

questions.question: A piece I'm drafting will run about 3,000 words — what does that mean for how it ships, and which day would part one go live on the blog?
questions.answer: Over the 2,400-word cap it must be split into a series; the blog publishes Thursday, so part one goes live Thursday.

questions.question: Why did we stop writing about the company as 'they' and start writing as 'I'?
questions.answer: Readers engaged 40% more with the founder's first-person personal essays, so the brand voice switched to first-person founder voice.

questions.question: Can I back a figure with a Statista summary?
questions.answer: No — claims with a statistic must link a primary source; secondary aggregators aren't acceptable.

questions.question: If I write a new cornerstone article, how many supporting posts should point at it and over what period are they expected?
questions.answer: Six cluster posts internally link up to each pillar page, at a cadence of one pillar per quarter.

questions.question: A two-year-old tutorial still pulls about 800 visits a month — do we kill it?
questions.answer: No — posts past 18 months are retired only if traffic is below 500 visits/month; at 800 it gets refreshed, not sunset.

questions.question: Who owns the rights to a piece written by an outside contributor, and where's the paperwork?
questions.answer: The guest contributor keeps copyright (granting a perpetual non-exclusive license); the contract template is in the legal folder.

questions.question: What's our retainer rate for freelance editors?
